title: /cost model-price lookup uses string-contains matching and hardcoded rates
description:
Steps to reproduce:
1. 

Expected: 
Actual: 

Dimension: KISS / Functional Correctness / YAGNI
Location: src/tui/app.rs:1029-1051 (/cost arm)
Violation: Price per token is computed via 'match self.model.as_str() { m if m.contains("opus") => ..., m if m.contains("sonnet") => ..., m if m.contains("haiku") => ..., _ => 0.0 }'. This (a) silently returns /bin/bash.00 for any non-Anthropic model (GPT-4, Gemini, DeepSeek, GLM, Qwen) — a wrong answer, not a missing one; (b) does not distinguish input vs output tokens (the assumption is all tokens are half-in-half-out); (c) hardcodes rates that change regularly; (d) collapses claude-3-opus-20240229 and claude-opus-4-x pricing into one rate.
Mandated Refactor: (1) Move pricing data to a src/pricing/models.toml loaded at startup, keyed by exact model id. (2) Define pub fn estimate_cost(model: &str, input_tokens: u64, output_tokens: u64) -> Option<f64>. (3) Return None and display 'unknown pricing for model X' rather than /bin/bash.00. (4) Track input and output tokens separately — the pipeline already has this breakdown.
